2b1ab06299308040a986fb71a7b5dcfb.characteristics: Email Lee sent to SLT (Bryon, Dee, Scott). Full assessment of EAB in relation to SPSI's marketing and enrollment operations completed. Checkpoint model outlined giving Moravian structure, pace, and accountability needed to keep close eye on EAB's alignment. Will work alongside marketing, enrollment, and SPSI leads to stand up checkpoints.

2b1ab06299308066acc9dcba920db5a0.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 1 - Due 11/30/25
2b1ab06299308066acc9dcba920db5a0.characteristics: By Nov 30, EAB must agree to the FY24-25 SPSI budget projections, volume projections, and cost-per-student model that Rhodes Advisors and SPSI leadership are finalizing. This will serve as the financial baseline for measuring performance and pacing. All SPSI programs fully reactivated across paid and organic journeys. Initial improvements to attribution and source tracking.

2b1ab06299308083b020ffce51a097d9.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 2 - Due 1/28/26
2b1ab06299308083b020ffce51a097d9.characteristics: Full implementation of Rhodes Advisors-provided creative, landing pages, and messaging. CRM workflows updated to match SPSI's journey mapping. Tracking alignment across EAB and the secondary vendor to enable February funnel clarity.

2b1ab062993080bcb96bd9d5871956bc.title: Moravian - Post February Oversight - Ongoing
2b1ab062993080bcb96bd9d5871956bc.characteristics: Weekly Performance Reviews: Rhodes Advisors and Moravian jointly review SPSI performance every week, with focus on lead volume, CPL, conversion quality, and pacing toward Fall 2025 goals. Financial Stewardship: All performance evaluations measured against SPSI budget and EAB-agreed projections. Pipeline Health Monitoring: SPSI must demonstrate consistent inquiry growth, improved lead quality, and stable funnel movement. Fail-Safe Protocol: If progress stalls, Moravian will escalate toward transition planning. Clear Ownership Model: SPSI's oversight of EAB centralized; Justine Bartholomew + Rhodes Advisors jointly own coordination.

2b1ab062993080bea721de915fa19782.title: Moravian - Checkpoint 3 - Due 2/26/26
2b1ab062993080bea721de915fa19782.characteristics: Clear progress in lead volume, CPL, and early conversion quality. Positive pacing toward Fall 2025 enrollment targets. Weekly program-level funnel reporting begins. February becomes the first unified view of lead/application/student performance. If progress insufficient, Moravian begins drafting vendor transition contracts for September 1 takeover.

2bfab0629930809899d9f3dc81908f8d.characteristics: Assessment of the labor market for Moravian's MS Cyber program: should they focus in NYC or Eastern PA?. Recommendation: Focus on Eastern PA.

2fdab0629930800bbe76ce327917e8a3.title: Develop and Deliver a Recommendation

330ab062993080e0884eebd3145f1121.title: MS Cybersecurity Program Viability Assessment - Packet
330ab062993080e0884eebd3145f1121.characteristics: Project: MS Cybersecurity Program Viability Assessment. Objective: Produce a concise, data-backed go/no-go recommendation on MS in Cybersecurity by evaluating market demand, enrollment feasibility, and competitive positioning in target geography. Demand Analysis: Quantify employer demand for cybersecurity talent in Greater Bethlehem/Eastern PA. Enrollment Feasibility Modeling: Develop directional enrollment model estimating realistic cohort potential. Competitive Landscape Assessment: Evaluate 5-8 directly competing master's-level cybersecurity programs. Performance Gap Diagnosis: Assess whether enrollment gaps are demand vs. execution problem. Go/No-Go Recommendation with supporting rationale.

33aab0629930807da2d7eb6c9fb65108.title: 30Credit MBA Financial Analysis
33aab0629930807da2d7eb6c9fb65108.characteristics: Evaluate Moravian's proposed restructuring of MBA from 36 to 30 credits. Focus on assessing financial sustainability and market viability of revised tuition price points at $21K, $25K, and $30K. Quantify revenue and enrollment implications of credit reduction. Determine enrollment thresholds required to offset per-student revenue loss. Assess whether each pricing scenario is defensible within competitive market. Inform a data-driven recommendation on optimal program pricing and positioning.

34bab062993080a78544d964f89f31a7.title: SPSI Program Call Scripts (for Archer Onboarding)

34bab062993080d29635dde714449118.title: SPSI SEM Salesforce Listviews
34bab062993080d29635dde714449118.characteristics: New List views (01_New to 06_Reachback) to help structure how SPSI SEM and team manage outreach and prioritize follow-up. Moravian uses 6 list views to organize SEM's pipeline. 1 - New Inquiries: New inquiries with fewer than 2 calls, highest priority. 2 - Uncontacted: Prospects and App Starts not contacted in 4 weeks. 3 - Contacted: All contacted prospects that are Verified and Engaged. 4 - Applications: Active applications for current and future start terms. 5 - Recent Movement: Prospects and applicants displaying recent activity. 6 - Reachback: Opportunities created more than 60 days ago.

34bab062993080e5a697ca86e96aa811.title: SPSI Program Guides (for Archer Onboarding)
34bab062993080e5a697ca86e96aa811.characteristics: Program Overview Guides listing: Credit, Cost, Value Proposition, Curriculum, Career Outcomes, Admissions Requirements. Multiple program guides created for Archer Onboarding.

351ab0629930808194a2ece92b60e2d6.characteristics: MBA-Aligned Certificate Recommendation Memo: strategic recommendation outlining the strongest certificate pathways to support the 30-credit MBA, including priority focus areas (starting with AI-aligned offerings), market rationale, positioning, and how certificates should function as stackable pathways into the degree. Project Plan + Go/No-Go Framework: practical execution plan defining timeline, ownership, operational requirements, and decision points to determine whether Moravian can realistically launch and impact Fall 2026 enrollment.

351ab062993080868d02c2e9995f51d4.title: Leadership Review & Decision
351ab062993080868d02c2e9995f51d4.characteristics: Leadership Review + Decision Support: presentation and discussion framework for Dee, Amy, Bryon, and academic leadership that supports alignment, decision-making, and next-step planning around certificate launch priorities and broader MBA portfolio strategy.
